content.problemStatement: Given n and m, calculate 11 +  22 + 33 + ... + nn modulo m.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers N and M.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the sum of the series 11 + 22 + 33 + ... + NN modulo M.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case, we calculate the sum of the series 11 + 22 + 33 + ... + NN modulo M.
We calculate the sum of the series 11 + 22 + 33 + ... + 10*10 modulo 100.
We calculate the sum of the series 11 + 22 + 33 + ... + 20*20 modulo 1000.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by using the formula for the sum of squares of the first N natural numbers, which is N*(N+1)*(2N+1)/6. However, the given series is 11 + 22 + 33 + ... + NN. We can rewrite this series as (1*1) + (2*2) + (3*3) + ... + (N*N). This is equivalent to the sum of squares of the first N natural numbers. We can then apply modular arithmetic to find the sum modulo M.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
